stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Ursula K. Le Guin’s 1968 novel A Wizard of Earthsea features two appendices.
  • The novel opens with a chronological list of major historical events in the fictional archipelago of Earthsea.
  • This timeline orients readers to the long arc of history before they enter the story.
  • The novel closes with a glossary defining terms in the invented Old Speech language.
  • That glossary lets readers revisit and reflect on the meaning of phrases the characters used.
stem: The student wants to contrast the purposes of the two appendices in A Wizard of Earthsea. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: A Wizard of Earthsea, a 1968 novel by Ursula K. Le Guin, features a chronological timeline at the start and a glossary at the end.
A Wizard of Earthsea’s two appendices, one opening and one closing the novel, each serve a purpose for readers.
A Wizard of Earthsea’s opening timeline orients readers to the long arc of Earthsea’s history, while the closing glossary lets them revisit and reflect on the meaning of phrases the characters used.
In 1968, author Ursula K. Le Guin published A Wizard of Earthsea, a novel featuring both an opening timeline and a closing glossary.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The sentence contrasts the purposes of the two appendices, noting that the opening timeline orients readers to Earthsea’s history, while the closing glossary lets them revisit the meaning of phrases. The word “while” signals a contrast between the purposes.

Choice A is incorrect. While the sentence mentions the two appendices, it doesn’t contrast their purposes. Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence mentions the two appendices and notes that each has a purpose, it doesn’t specify what those purposes are or how they contrast. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence mentions the two appendices, it doesn’t contrast their purposes.
